steps.title: Set up the equation with consecutive integers
steps.content:
Let the three consecutive integers be $n$, $n+1$, and $n+2$, where $n+2$ is the greatest. The condition states: (sum of other two) minus (greatest) equals 48.
$$(n + (n+1)) - (n+2) = 48$$

steps.title: Simplify and solve
steps.content:
Expanding and combining like terms:
$$n + n + 1 - n - 2 = 48$$
$$n - 1 = 48$$
$$n = 49$$
The three consecutive integers are 49, 50, and 51. The greatest of the three integers is $51$.
